content:
A high-level summary artifact (for example, CONTINUITY-ARCHIVE-LOG-SESSION-YYYYMMDDhhmm-v1_0).
Raw transcripts or condensed narratives as needed.
A SEAL block describing dependencies and next actions.

$ARCHIVE_ROOT/worlds stores world states, each in its own subdirectory keyed by a stable world ID. Within each world directory, maintain separate artifacts for ontology, geography, timeline, and open questions.

$ARCHIVE_ROOT/characters stores character sheets and arc logs, cross-linked to world IDs and story waves.

$ARCHIVE_ROOT/specs stores both current and historical specs, including deprecated versions. Current versions are clearly marked via version numbers and manifest entries.

$ARCHIVE_ROOT/meta stores cross-cutting indices, manifests, and continuity maps.

4.2 Timestamping and Versioning

Use a consistent timestamp convention for IDs and file suffixes, for example YYYYMMDDhhmm. VERSION components such as v0_1, v1_0, v1_1 are used to mark evolution within a logical line of a specification or narrative artifact.
